ogTitle: Blueys Beach vs Boambee East: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blueys Beach and Boambee East.
heading: Blueys Beach vs Boambee East — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blueys Beach, New South Wales and Boambee East, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Boambee East has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Blueys Beach (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blueys Beach or Boambee East safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blueys Beach has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Boambee East scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blueys Beach scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Boambee East scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blueys Beach or Boambee East?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Boambee East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
